rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: clearest photo first word catalogue; Baby Touch Words is touch led naming.
include_rationale: Finding words at sixteen to eighteen months often means pointing at a clear photo and hearing you name it. First 100 Words keeps pages chunky and pictures uncluttered. Best when you want one photo catalogue you can open for two minutes between meals without a story plot.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #2 over #3: touch led first words; Barefoot is illustrated word scenes.
include_rationale: Some toddlers need a finger job before they will stay for a word. Baby Touch Words pairs a patch with a label so pointing has something to do. Choose when photo catalogues already seem dry and you want touch led naming without a long story.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #3 over #4: flap naming classic; Spot is seek and name.
include_rationale: Flaps turn naming into a job with a clear next move. Dear Zoo keeps the animal list familiar without a photo catalogue. Best when first word catalogues already cover pointing and you want a short story style naming pattern with flaps.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: seek and name Spot; Hungry Caterpillar is food naming.
include_rationale: Seek and name keeps first words tied to a game they already understand. Spot flaps give a clear place to look next on each page. Choose when Dear Zoo already covers animal flaps and you want a seek story with the same naming job.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #5 over #6: illustrated word scenes; Ladybird faces is face pointing.
include_rationale: Photo pages are not the only way into first words. Barefoot scenes keep labels clear while the art stays calmer than a dense catalogue. Best when Priddy photos already live on the shelf and you want an illustrated word book for quieter sofa minutes.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #6 over #7: second Ladybird face naming; Puppy is texture naming.
include_rationale: Face words show up in every mirror and photo on the phone. Baby Touch Faces keeps naming concrete without a dense hundred word spread. Choose when Baby Touch Words already covers general labels and you want a second Ladybird focused on faces.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #7 over #8: texture naming; Priddy Animals is photo animals.
include_rationale: Touch patches keep describing words going when photo catalogues lose them. Dinosaur pages give a different theme from everyday object lists on the sofa. Choose when Ladybird touch books already cover one line and you want Usborne style texture naming.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #8 over #9: photo baby faces; Priddy Animals is animal photos.
include_rationale: Face pages still help first words when catalogues seem dense. Global Babies keeps the job to looking and naming faces without a hundred object grid. Choose when Ladybird Faces already covers high contrast art and you want photo babies instead.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #9 over #10: second Priddy texture words; Hello Baby Faces is faces theme.
include_rationale: Texture pages buy you another minute of attention when plain photos lose them. See, Touch, Feel First Words keeps labels simple while fingers stay busy on the page. Best as the second Priddy when First 100 Words already covers photo pointing.

rating_source: Publisher or bookshop PDP; on-page review aggregate not published
rank_rationale: #10: second Fiona Watt texture theme after Dinosaur.
include_rationale: Describing words arrive beside object names when fingers meet a patch. That is Not My Puppy keeps the pattern short and familiar on the sofa. Choose when Dinosaur already covers one touchy feely theme and you want a puppy version too.
